steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the overall structure of the text. Read for the big-picture two-part movement: what does the text do first, and what does it do second?

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The text summarizes the plot of a franchise movie, then explains how the plot is continued in a later movie in that franchise.: No plot is summarized; the passage never describes what happens in any movie. ✗
  • Choice B — The text explains why some people enjoyed a particular franchise movie, then considers why other people disliked it.: The text does not split enjoyment vs. dislike of one film; it defends franchise movies broadly. ✗
  • Choice C — The text describes audiences' reactions to a movie that is part of a larger franchise, then presents some critics' reactions to that movie.: Audience reactions are never described, and critics appear only briefly as part of the counter-argument, not as a second structural section. ✗
  • Choice D — The text discusses a common criticism of franchise movies, then argues that the criticism is mostly undeserved.: The text opens by naming the criticism (franchise movies are predictable), then uses Spider-Man, the critical concession, and Logan as evidence that the criticism is overstated. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
